build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors.

requirements.description: The player character moves in a top-down view and fires a gun that shoots projectiles toward the cursor, with enemies firing dense bullet patterns that must be dodged. Score 0 if there is no shooting or bullet patterns. Score 0.5 at most if shooting exists but enemies fire single projectiles with no pattern density. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A dodge-roll ability grants brief invincibility frames, allowing the player to pass through bullets. The roll has a visible animation and short cooldown. Score 0 if no dodge mechanic exists. Score 0.5 at most if dodging exists but grants no invincibility or has no cooldown. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Rooms are connected in a roguelike structure: clearing enemies opens exits, and the player chooses which room to enter next. Death restarts the run from floor 1. Score 0 if there is no room structure or roguelike progression. Score 0.5 at most if rooms exist but there is no permadeath or choice between exits. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Gun pickups found between rooms offer weapon swaps with distinct firing patterns (shotgun spread, railgun pierce, bouncing orbs, rapid fire). Score 0 if only one weapon exists throughout. Score 0.5 at most if weapons can be swapped but all fire identically. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The player has limited health (hearts or HP) that depletes when hit by bullets, with visible damage feedback and a game-over state when health reaches zero. Score 0 if the player is invincible or has no health system. Score 0.5 at most if health exists but damage has no visual feedback. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least 3 floors with 5-7 rooms each, with different room themes, enemy compositions, and increasing difficulty per floor. Score 0 if only one floor exists. Score 0.5 at most if floors exist but are visually and mechanically identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 5 distinct gun types with different projectile behaviors, fire rates, and tactical uses. Score 0 if only one gun exists. Score 0.5 at most if guns differ only in damage with identical projectile behavior. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 4 distinct enemy types per floor with different bullet patterns (radial, aimed, spiral, random burst) and movement behaviors. Score 0 if only one enemy type exists. Score 0.5 at most if enemies look different but fire identical patterns. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Each floor ends with a boss that has at least 3 distinct attack phases with escalating bullet pattern complexity. Score 0 if no floor bosses exist. Score 0.5 at most if a boss exists but has only one attack pattern. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A run-end screen shows rooms cleared, enemies defeated, guns found, and floor reached, with a victory screen for completing all floors. Score 0 if the game ends abruptly with no summary. Score 0.5 at most if only a single stat is shown. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Normal startup shows a styled title screen with the game name, a Start Run control, and gothic or dungeon-themed imagery. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse text on a blank background. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The flow connects title to room combat to room transitions to boss encounters to floor interstitials without restarting the executable. Score 0 if major transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if transitions exist but break visually. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The HUD clearly shows health, current gun, room map or progress indicator, and floor number in readable non-overlapping regions. Score 0 if the player cannot determine game state. Score 0.5 at most if information is present but overlapping or unreadable. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Player actions produce visible feedback: dodge-roll shows motion blur or ghost, hits on enemies flash, bullet patterns are clearly visible against backgrounds, and room clears trigger a celebration effect. Score 0 if actions happen silently. Score 0.5 at most if only one feedback type exists. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Art direction is coherent across title, rooms, enemies, bullets, guns, HUD, and interstitials with a consistent gothic-dungeon palette and style. Score 0 if mostly default controls or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

The player character, enemies, bosses, bullets, guns, and room tiles use real sprite art with distinct designs and readable silhouettes. Score 0 if primary gameplay elements are unstyled rectangles. Score 0.5 at most if sprites exist but are inconsistent in style. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation brings rooms to life: dodge-roll animation, bullet pattern motion, enemy attack windups, boss phase transitions, and explosion effects. Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if only one element animates. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI panels use styled themed widgets with gothic frames, custom fonts, or dungeon-styled elements rather than raw Godot defaults. Score 0 if all UI is default Label/Button. Score 0.5 at most if some panels are styled but others remain raw defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
